title: [3/3] [MooreToCore] Declare malloc as an LLVM function
body:
Fix class allocation lowering to call `malloc` through an LLVM symbol
instead of a Func-dialect declaration.

`ClassNewOpConversion` was emitting an `llvm.call @malloc` while the
cached declaration still came from `func.func`, which caused LLVM
verification to reject the call target. Switch the allocation path to
use `LLVM::lookupOrCreateMallocFn` and emit the call directly as an
LLVM call to a valid `llvm.func`.

This keeps `class.new` legal through `convert-to-llvm` and matches the
earlier vtable lowering work, which already moved function-addressable
entities into LLVM dialect.
